questions.question: Explain the difference between public and private IP addresses with examples.
keyPoints: Public IP addresses are globally unique and routable on the internet
Private IP addresses are used within local networks and not routable on internet
NAT is used to map private to public addresses

questions.question: What is subnetting? Why is it used?
keyPoints: Subnetting divides a larger network into smaller subnets
It improves network performance and security

questions.question: Calculate the network address, first usable host, last usable host, and broadcast address for IP 192.168.50.75/26.
Explain the IPv4 address classes (A, B, C) with their default subnet masks and typical use cases.
keyPoints: Each class has specific bit allocations for network and host portions

questions.question: What is CIDR notation? Convert subnet mask 255.255.255.248 to CIDR notation and calculate usable hosts.
keyPoints: CIDR shows network bits with slash notation

questions.question: Define and differentiate between network address and broadcast address.
keyPoints: Network address: first IP in subnet, host bits all 0, identifies the network
Broadcast address: last IP in subnet, host bits all 1, used to send to all hosts
Neither can be assigned to hosts

questions.question: What is VLSM? What advantage does it offer over traditional subnetting?
keyPoints: VLSM stands for Variable Length Subnet Masking
Allows subnets of different sizes within the same network
Enables efficient allocation based on actual host requirements

questions.question: Given the network 172.16.0.0/16, design a subnetting scheme to accommodate the following departments: Engineering (500 hosts), Sales (200 hosts), HR (50 hosts), and Management (25 hosts). Calculate subnet masks and address ranges for each.
keyPoints: Show proper VLSM allocation without overlap

questions.question: Explain the process of subnetting a Class C network 192.168.1.0/24 into 4 equal subnets. List network address, subnet mask, range of usable IPs, and broadcast address for each subnet.
keyPoints: Borrow 2 bits from host portion to create 4 subnets (/26)

questions.question: Discuss the limitations of IPv4 and explain how IPv4 address exhaustion is addressed through NAT, CIDR, and IPv6.
keyPoints: IPv4 has only 32-bit addresses (~4.3 billion), exhaustion occurred
NAT allows multiple private IPs to share single public IP
CIDR eliminates class restrictions, efficient address allocation
Private IP ranges defined to conserve public addresses

questions.question: A company has been assigned network 10.0.0.0/8. The company has 3 branch offices. Branch A needs 2 million hosts, Branch B needs 500,000 hosts, and Branch C needs 100,000 hosts. Design an efficient subnetting scheme using VLSM.
keyPoints: Verify no overlap and efficient space usage
